Transcript:

Doing physical therapy for your dog's arthritis actually makes it worse.

Here's what to do instead.

This is the most natural way to flush out doggy joint pain.

When Kevin was eight, he started to slow down.

He went from pulling on his leash to dragging behind me.

He needed help getting into the car and he even began to limp.

I tried everything.

Glucosamine chews, acupuncture, massages, heating pads, memory foam beds, and even physical therapy, but nothing worked.

His pain only got worse.

And when I gave him medications, they had all these nasty side effects.

Vomiting, loss of appetite, and even depression.

After a few months, Kevin couldn't even climb the stairs or jump into bed anymore without help.

It was gut-wrenching to see my fur baby's happiness and energy gone.

And that's when I asked my vet, if this was your pet, what would you do?

And his answer broke my heart.

I booked the dreaded appointment for the following week, but I refused to believe that this was really the end.

I spent that entire night Googling ways to help Kevin.

And I'm so glad that I did because that's when I stumbled upon this breakthrough study that changed everything.

I learned that there's this natural lubricant in doggy joints known as synovial fluid.

And when it dries out, it wears down your dog's cartilage, causing bones to grind against each other.

Yet most doggy joint solutions don't focus on this synovial fluid.

They only focus on repairing the damaged cartilage, which is kind of like fixing an engine without bothering to change the oil.

And that's a problem.

Because as your dog ages, their synovial fluid dries out, leading to fiery joint-on-joint friction, unbearable pain, and doggy depression.

Researchers call this synovial drying.

And if your dog is already limping, it's a sign that they're suffering from it right now.

But here's the silver lining.

There's a simple way to rehydrate your dog's joints and restore their synovial fluid levels.

It's all thanks to this five-second water bowl ritual created by a world-renowned vet named Dr. Randy Aronson.

By the time I'd found it, it had already helped thousands of dogs.

So of course, I had to give it a try, and I was shocked by what happened next.

On day one of using the five-second water bowl ritual, Kevin wagged his tail for the first time in weeks and was able to walk longer.

Day two, he played tug-of-war with me.

Day five, he started walking up the stairs on his own and jumping into bed again.

And when day six arrived, I canceled that dreaded appointment with the vet.

Kevin went from limping to running in two weeks.

It didn't involve surgeries, acupuncture, or glucosamine chews, but making one small change.

And I'm happy to say it's one year later and Kevin's jumping, running, and playing like he did when he was a puppy.

It's like Kevin and I have been given a second chance.

But it breaks my heart to think about other loving dog parents losing precious time with their fur babies when it could have been avoided.

So if your dog is slowing down or showing any signs of joint pain or discomfort, do yourself and your pup a favor and check out this free presentation that Dr. Randy Aronson's created.

He'll show you his five-second water bowl ritual to flush out doggy joint pain for good.

So your dog can run, jump, and play like a puppy again and get back to feeling like they're happy-go-lucky selves in as little as a few days from now.

So click the button below this video to see Dr. Aronson's five-second water bowl ritual.

Take it from me, it could literally save your dog's life.
